title: High-Quality Source Taxonomy — Real-Asset Equity Research
description: The knowledge base of permitted, quality-ranked sources. Agents may ONLY cite from sources resolvable to one of these tiers. Explicitly EXCLUDED: Reddit, StockTwits, retail forums, anonymous blogs, promotional newsletters, paid stock-promotion sites, generic content farms. Where a source is paywalled beyond budget, it is listed as 'reference_only' (use the free proxy noted).

tier_definitions.T1_primary_filing: Company's own regulated disclosures and technical documents. Highest authority for company-specific facts.
tier_definitions.T2_regulator_or_gov: Government / regulator / multilateral. Highest authority for industry, commodity, policy, macro facts.
tier_definitions.T3_industry_specialist: Trade bodies and recognized industry analysts. Authority for industry structure & dynamics.
tier_definitions.T4_quality_media: Reputable journalism with editorial standards. Good for catalysts, context, breaking developments.
tier_definitions.T5_api_aggregator: Structured-data vendors. Convenient but record underlying origin where known.
tier_definitions.T6_model_estimate: Agent's own calculation/inference. Always show method.

canada.name: SEDAR+
canada.note: CRITICAL for mining small caps — most junior miners list TSX/TSX-V.

australia.name: ASX Announcements
australia.note: CRITICAL for rare earths, lithium, uranium juniors.

uk_eu.name: company registry / regulated-info via exchange (Euronext, Deutsche Börse, Nordics)

global_generic.name: Company investor-relations site
global_generic.note: Often the richest real-asset detail (mine plans, PPA terms) lives in IR decks.

commodities_minerals.name: USGS Mineral Commodity Summaries
commodities_minerals.covers: 90+ minerals: production, reserves, price, net-import-reliance, by country

commodities_minerals.name: British Geological Survey — World Mineral Statistics
EU Critical Raw Materials Act / RMIS

energy_power_grid.name: IEA reports
energy_power_grid.covers: electricity, grids, electrification, critical minerals for energy, capacity outlooks

energy_power_grid.name: US EIA
energy_power_grid.covers: US energy data, generation, capacity, prices

energy_power_grid.name: Ember
energy_power_grid.tier_note: borderline T2/T3, high quality, open data

macro_trade_policy.name: World Bank Data / Commodity Markets 'Pink Sheet'

emerging_markets.name: national statistics offices & central banks (Indonesia BPS/BI, Kazakhstan, Philippines PSA)
emerging_markets.use: under-researched region data, often local-language => translate
emerging_markets.note: Translation of local-language regulatory/news = a genuine alpha edge per your #14.

nuclear_fuel_cycle.name: IAEA (Power Reactor Information System / data)
nuclear_fuel_cycle.covers: reactor fleet, fuel cycle, HALEU context
nuclear_fuel_cycle.note: Bottleneck in nuclear is conversion/enrichment/fabrication capacity, not ore — map names to that chokepoint.

nuclear_fuel_cycle.name: World Nuclear Association — fuel cycle pages

water_infrastructure.name: national water regulators (e.g. Ofwat UK, US EPA)

logistics_ports_rail_shipping.name: Baltic Exchange indices (headline levels)
logistics_ports_rail_shipping.note: Headline indices public; granular data paid.

logistics_ports_rail_shipping.name: port-authority throughput data + national rail regulators

defense_industrial.name: government procurement: USAspending.gov
defense_industrial.covers: US federal contract awards to specific (often small) suppliers
defense_industrial.priority: HIGH — structured award data almost nobody screens

free_or_partly_free.name: industry trade associations (e.g. Copper Development Assoc, Silver Institute, Lithium producers' disclosures)

reference_only_paywalled_beyond_budget.name: Wood Mackenzie
reference_only_paywalled_beyond_budget.free_proxy: USGS cost data + company technical reports for AISC; IEA for power

reference_only_paywalled_beyond_budget.name: Benchmark Mineral Intelligence
reference_only_paywalled_beyond_budget.free_proxy: USGS + producer disclosures + World Bank Pink Sheet

reference_only_paywalled_beyond_budget.name: Fastmarkets / Argus
reference_only_paywalled_beyond_budget.free_proxy: Shanghai Metals Market free tier; USGS notes Argus prices in MCS (citable secondhand via USGS T2)

reference_only_paywalled_beyond_budget.name: UxC / TradeTech
reference_only_paywalled_beyond_budget.free_proxy: Cameco posts indicative spot/term; Sprott Physical Uranium Trust NAV; Numerco public spot

regional_underresearched.name: regional/local-language business press (translate)
regional_underresearched.use: per #14 edge: stories not in English-language coverage

T4_quality_media.usage_rule: T4 supports catalysts/context only. Never the SOLE basis for a number — corroborate to T1/T2.

primary.name: EODHD Fundamentals Data Feed (student rate ~30 CHF)
primary.covers: global fundamentals, financials, insider (US), holders, screener, news+sentiment, macro
primary.agent_native: MCP server (75 tools) + OpenAPI 3.1 + Agent Skills

free_supplementary.name: SEC EDGAR API (data.sec.gov)
free_supplementary.use: authoritative US fundamentals + filings, corroborates EODHD

free_supplementary.name: OpenBB Platform
free_supplementary.use: aggregation layer that itself wraps many free providers; good agent tooling

free_supplementary.name: Yahoo Finance (unofficial)
free_supplementary.tier_note: convenience only, treat as T5 low-quality, corroborate

discovery_feeds_event_triggers.description: CHANGE-detection sources — the heart of non-consensus discovery. Static fundamentals converge on the same names; these feeds surface re-rating EVENTS in public regulator/procurement/registry files BEFORE sell-side reacts. Map each to discovery_profile.event_trigger.trigger_type. All free or freemium.

permits_and_capacity.name: mining permit / environmental-assessment registries (national & state)
permits_and_capacity.note: A granted permit is a binary re-rating event sitting in a public regulator file.

permits_and_capacity.name: grid connection / interconnection queues (e.g. national TSO, FERC, ENTSO-E)
company technical reports & quarterly activity reports (NI 43-101, JORC, ASX 5B)

government_contracts_and_procurement.name: USAspending.gov
government_contracts_and_procurement.priority: HIGH — small-supplier awards are public, structured, almost unscreened

trade_export_controls_stockpiles.name: UN Comtrade
trade_export_controls_stockpiles.note: See a physical flow shift before it hits any company's revenue.

trade_export_controls_stockpiles.name: export-control & licensing announcements (e.g. China MOFCOM gallium/germanium/graphite; US BIS Entity List; strategic stockpile notices)
trade_export_controls_stockpiles.priority: HIGH — policy flows that move specific names

innovation_capability.name: USPTO PatFT / EPO Espacenet
innovation_capability.note: A spike in a small firm's filings in a specific separation/processing tech is a forward capability signal.

financing_events.name: filings disclosing refinancing / new facility / bond issue (EDGAR/SEDAR+/RNS)

commodity_price_sources_quick_reference.note: These fill real_asset_block.commodity_price_sensitivity. At budget, expect quality B at best (World Bank/USGS) down to C/D (proxies). ALWAYS flag.
uranium: Sprott Physical Uranium Trust NAV/holdings (T1, free)
rare_earths_oxides: USGS MCS (cites Argus FOB-China, T2, free, but lagged)
lithium_graphite_cobalt_nickel: World Bank Pink Sheet (T2, free, monthly)
copper_aluminium_zinc: LME via World Bank Pink Sheet (T2, free)
grid_equipment_transformers_cable: no clean spot price — use order-book/backlog disclosures (T1) + ENTSO-E/IEA grid-investment outlooks (T2)
water: no commodity spot — use regulated rate cases / tariff filings (T1/T2) + utility capex programs
specialty_chemicals_intermediates: mostly contract-priced — use producer realized prices (T1) + ICIS/C&EN headline commentary (T4) + trade-flow proxy via UN Comtrade (T2)

excluded_sources_hard_block: anonymous Substack/blogspot with no disclosed author
X/Twitter as a primary fact source (may be a lead, never a citation)
AI-generated aggregator pages with no primary source
